sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, PDF page 33 (printed page 6, section header 'EXTRA USEFUL QUESTIONS:'), bottom of page, rendered image: "5) The kernel of the operating system remains in primary memory because / a) It manages interrupt calls / b) It is mostly used or called". Nothing follows on that page (page number '6' is next) and PDF page 34 opens with "6) Difference between == and. equals?". The paper prints exactly TWO options for this question; there are no c) or d) to recover. Option b) is printed in bold+underline, which is this document's marking for the keyed-correct answer (same convention as q1 'c) The movement of pages in and out of the memory' and q4 'a) Wi-Fi' on the same page).
note: Two options is faithful to the source, not an extraction drop (cf. q9 'How to delete a table in a database? a) Drop b) Delete' in the same document, also two options); the key is in fact bold-underlined in the paper, so keyProvenance 'derived' understates it - key untouched in this pass, flagged for the key pass.

sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, PDF page 35 (printed page 8), rendered image: "13) What is the main difference between DDCMP and SDLC? / a. DDCMP does not need special hardware to final the beginning of a message [bold + underlined] / b. DDCMP has a message header / c. SDLC has an IP address / d. SDLC does not use CRC".
note: The paper itself prints 'to final the beginning of a message'; the typo is in the source (it is the bold-underlined keyed answer), so it is kept verbatim per fidelity rule.

sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, PDF page 33 (printed page 6), rendered image: "1) Page traffic describes / a) The pages in memory / b) The papers required to be brought is a given page request / c) The movement of pages in and out of the memory [bold + underlined] / d) The number of pages of executing programs loaded in memory".
note: Option B is printed word-for-word as we ship it, 'papers' and 'is a given page request' included; the garble is the paper's own, kept verbatim.

sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, PDF page 21 (printed page 21), rendered image: "Q94: What is the correct answer when using AND gate true table in the digital circuits design: / A. 1 AND 1 is 0 / B. 1 AND 0 is 1 / C. 1 AND 1 is 0 / D. 1 AND 1 is 1 [bold + underlined]".
note: The duplicated distractor is in the PAPER, not our extraction: A and C are printed identically. D is bold-underlined (keyed correct) and remains uniquely correct, so the item is still answerable; no repair possible without inventing an option, and no source defect that blocks answering, so it stays as shipped.

sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, PDF page 20 (printed page 20), rendered image: "Q91: The main benefit of spreading multiple tablespaces across disks is to: / A. Reduce disk fragmentation / B. Increase tablespace sizes / C. Minimize disk I/O connection [bold + underlined] / D. None of the above".
note: The paper literally prints 'Minimize disk I/O connection' (not 'contention'); the wording error is the source's own and is kept verbatim per fidelity rule.
